summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Simple harmonic motion'?

"Simple harmonic motion" means: Oscillation where F = -kx and x(t) = A cos(ωt + φ); characterized by constant period independent of amplitude. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Angular frequency'?

"Angular frequency" means: ω = √(k/m) = 2πf = 2π/T; determines how fast the oscillation occurs. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Amplitude'?

"Amplitude" means: A; the maximum displacement from equilibrium; determines the total energy E = ½kA². The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Phase constant (φ)'?

"Phase constant (φ)" means: The initial phase angle that determines the starting position and velocity of the oscillation at t = 0. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Velocity and acceleration in SHM'?

"Velocity and acceleration in SHM" means: v(t) = -Aω sin(ωt + φ), a(t) = -Aω² cos(ωt + φ) = -ω²x; acceleration is proportional and opposite to displacement. The other options describe different concepts.

summary_sections.title: Special Topics
summary_sections.content_html:
Important Concepts

Which of the following best defines "Angular frequency"

"Angular frequency" is defined as: ω = √(k/m) = 2πf = 2π/T; determines how fast the oscillation occurs. The other options describe different concepts.
